news.summary: A recent report reveals that the global population has reached approximately 7.9 billion people. The study also indicates that around 60% of the world's population has access to the internet, which amounts to over 4.7 billion individuals. Additionally, it was found that the average person spends about 6.5 hours online each day. The report highlights the increasing reliance on technology and the internet in daily life.
news.headline: Trump hints at 80% tariff on China before negotiations.

news.summary: Galaxy Digital has received approval from the US Securities and Exchange Commission to redomicile to the United States and is set to list on the Nasdaq stock exchange by mid-May. Shareholder approval is needed at a special meeting on May 9 to finalize the move to the US state of Delaware for business-friendly regulations. The company plans to trade on Nasdaq under the ticker symbol GLXY once all approvals are secured. This move comes in the midst of increasing institutional interest in digital assets and crypto firms seeking stock market listings. Other crypto firms like Circle, Coinbase, and BitGo are also considering bank charters to further integrate with traditional financial institutions. This highlights the blend between crypto firms and traditional financial institutions, signaling the maturation of the crypto industry.
news.headline: Galaxy Digital approved to redomicile in US, paving way for Nasdaq listing in May pending TSE and shareholder approval. Crypto firms eye move into traditional financial institutions.

news.summary: On Friday, Tesla's stock surpassed both the 200-day moving average and recent highs, indicating a strong buy signal prior to the upcoming June robotaxi launch. This move suggests a promising outlook for investors as the company prepares to introduce its autonomous taxi service. The positive momentum in Tesla's stock price is seen as a favorable indicator for potential growth in the near future.
news.headline: Tesla stock shows buy signal as it surpasses 200-day moving average & recent highs before June robotaxi launch.

The warning comes from an article on Investor's Business Daily, highlighting the need to approach Netflix and Take-Two Interactive with care. It is crucial to consider all aspects of the market before investing in these companies.
news.headline: Caution advised on Netflix and Take-Two due to market factors despite negative headlines. - Investor's Business Daily

news.summary: In Boston, a recent study by Pioneer Institute presents findings from the IRA Medicare Drug Access Tracker, a tool created by Dr. William Smith and Dr. Robert Popovian. This tool aims to monitor the effects of the Inflation Reduction Act (IRA) on drug pricing. Surprisingly, the study reveals that federal drug price controls, designed to lower costs for seniors, are actually causing an increase in prescription drug prices for Medicare beneficiaries. The analysis focuses on nine frequently prescribed medications, showing a significant rise in costs due to the IRA's provisions. This highlights a concerning trend where the intended benefits of reducing out-of-pocket expenses are not being realized, impacting the affordability of essential medications for seniors.
news.headline: New tool by Pioneer Institute finds drug price controls leading to higher costs for Medicare patients, with 9 drugs showing increased prices.

news.summary: In Las Vegas on May 9, 2025, Verb Technology Company, Inc. (Nasdaq: VERB) disclosed its plans to host a conference call regarding the financial outcomes of the first quarter of 2025. Scheduled for Tuesday, May 13, 2025, at 1:00 p.m. Eastern time (10:00 a.m. Pacific time), the call will feature CEO Rory J. Cutaia. Noteworthy business segments of VERB include MARKET.live, VANITYPrescribed, GoodGirlRx, and the GO FUND YOURSELF TV Show. The official financial results will be shared in a press release before the scheduled call.
news.headline: Verb Technology Company, Inc. will host Q1 2025 earnings call on May 13 at 1:00 pm ET. CEO will discuss financial results.

news.summary: Harvard Bioscience (HBIO) is set to report Q1 2025 earnings with analysts estimating an EPS of $-0.04. Past performance shows beating EPS by $0.01 in the previous release led to a 6.55% share price drop. As of May 8, shares were trading at $0.3046, down 90.61% over 52 weeks. Analysts rate Harvard Bioscience as Outperform, with 1 rating and undisclosed one-year price target. Investors will focus on guidance for future quarters, as it may impact the stock price more than earnings beats or misses. Market sentiment suggests long-term shareholders are bearish heading into the earnings release.
news.headline: Harvard Bioscience to report earnings of $-0.04 per share, its stock down 90.61% over last 52 weeks. Analysts rate it Outperform.

news.summary: Resecurity (USA), a prominent cybersecurity solutions provider based in Los Angeles, has introduced Resecurity One, a cutting-edge cybersecurity platform aimed at transforming organizations' cybersecurity strategies. Resecurity One integrates Digital Risk Management, Cyber Threat Intelligence, Endpoint Protection, Identity Protection, Supply Chain Risk Monitoring, and xDR capabilities to offer a holistic defense against advancing cyber threats. This innovative platform is set to enhance cybersecurity measures with a unified solution.
news.headline: Resecurity introduces Resecurity One, a next-gen cybersecurity platform with various capabilities for comprehensive cyber protection.

news.summary: The shares of Trade Desk, Inc. (NASDAQ: TTD) have surged after the company announced its first-quarter results surpassing expectations. The company posted earnings of 33 cents per share, exceeding the estimated 25 cents, with revenue hitting $616.02 million, surpassing the expected $584.27 million. The forecast for second-quarter revenue stands at a minimum of $682 million, while analysts anticipated $685.04 million. KeyBanc analyst Justin Patterson raised the price target from $67 to $80 with an Overweight rating, citing the fourth-quarter miss as a one-time occurrence. For more details, refer to the source mentioned in the article.
news.headline: Trade Desk, Inc (TTD) reports Q1 earnings of 33 cents/share, beating estimate of 25 cents and revenue of $616.02M, surpassing $584.27M. Analyst sees 20% growth, raises price target to $80.

news.summary: The US Postal Service reported a controllable loss of $848 million for the second quarter of fiscal year 2025, compared to $317 million for the same period last year. The net loss for the quarter was $3.3 billion, an increase from $1.5 billion last year. The losses were due to non-controllable factors like workers' compensation adjustments and higher expenses. Despite this, total operating revenue remained steady at $19.7 billion. First-Class Mail revenue increased by 1.0%, while Shipping and Packages revenue increased by 0.7%. Marketing Mail revenue decreased by 1.4%. Total operating expenses were $23.1 billion, up by 8.3% from last year. The Postal Service is working to control costs, increase revenues, and modernize infrastructure to achieve financial sustainability. They have saved $116 million in transportation costs and 10 million work hours through their strategic plan. The plan aims to innovate, grow revenue, work efficiently, and uphold their universal service mission.
news.headline: USPS Q2 FY 2025: Controllable loss at $848M, net loss $3.3B, total revenue $19.7B, incurring $1.8B expenses. Major factors include non-cash workers' comp adjustments & volume declines in mail categories. Delivery network efficiencies & growth noted, strategic plan saving costs.

news.summary: Following the first quarter results of Hut 8 Corp. (NASDAQ: HUT), analysts have maintained their rating and price forecast. The company reported an EPS of $(1.30), falling below the estimated $(0.24), while sales were $21.82 million, missing the estimated $33.28 million. Analyst Mike Colonnese from H.C. Wainwright & Co. kept a Buy rating with a price target of $25. Despite the weak results, Hut 8’s stock rose by 12% on positive updates regarding their High-Performance Computing (HPC)/AI colocation pipeline. Colonnese noted the company's shift to an energy infrastructure platform, with a 2.6 GW HPC/AI pipeline, which could lead to a re-rating upon securing stable HPC contracts. The analyst also expressed optimism about the company's legacy mining (American Bitcoin) and future prospects.
news.headline: Analysts bullish on Hut 8 despite Q1 miss; EPS at $(1.30), sales $21.82M (est. $33.28M). Stock up 12% on HPC/AI bets. Buy rating, $25 target.

news.summary: The Hong Kong Monetary Authority has revealed plans to conduct a tender for 10-year RMB Institutional Government Bonds, with an allotted total amount of RMB1.0 billion. This tender is scheduled for May 13, 2025.
news.headline: Hong Kong set to auction 10-year RMB Institutional Govt Bonds totaling RMB1.0 billion on May 13, 2025.

news.summary: Hong Kong Monetary Authority has revealed its plan to issue RMB 1.5 billion worth of 2-year government bonds on May 13, 2025, under the Infrastructure Bond Programme. The upcoming tender presents an opportunity for investors looking to participate in this initiative.
news.headline: Hong Kong to auction RMB 1.5B gov't bonds on May 13, 2025, under Infrastructure Bond Programme.

news.summary: At the recent Chainalysis Links conference, it was discussed that Ripple's XRP Ledger is transforming DeFi payments by pioneering stablecoin advancements and practical financial use cases. The conference highlighted the significant impact of XRP Ledger in the DeFi space.
news.headline: XRP Ledger redefines DeFi payments with stablecoin tech, real-world finance apps - as discussed at Chainalysis Links event.

news.summary: Chaska, MN - Today, Mike Lindell Media Corporation (OTC: MLMC) announced the appointment of attorney Daniel J. Eastman as General Counsel. With over 35 years of legal and regulatory experience, Mr. Eastman will oversee the Company's legal affairs, corporate governance, and risk management strategies amid increased litigation activity and industry scrutiny. His background includes roles as an SEC legal staff intern, state securities regulator, and corporate lawyer with expertise in Federal and state securities law compliance and securities fraud defense. Mr. Eastman has served as CEO, board member, and provided counsel on numerous financial restructurings. Known for his adept handling of legal crises and enhancing corporate compliance in dynamic environments, he brings a wealth of experience to his new role.
news.headline: New General Counsel Daniel J. Eastman appointed by Mike Lindell Media Corp. (OTC: MLMC), with over 35 years of legal experience.
